summary: The CELL BLOCK Z codebase has TWO fundamentally different crowd/population systems: the jail **mass-crowd** (excellent, sophisticated) and the city **peds** (basic, limited). The jail system is a three-tier population engine: (1) **ambient tier** using typed arrays + hidden movement (src/entities/ambientstate.js), (2) **instanced-mesh tier** for ~360 nearby bodies (src/entities/crowd.js), and (3) **face-rig tier** for up to 40 closest agents with full character rigs + skinning + interaction (CBZ.makeCharacter). Each tier has distinct rendering: ambient uses Points clouds (density view) or InstancedMesh boxes (overview), instanced uses 11 per-agent meshes (torso, head, hair, 4 limbs, eyes, mouth, valuable), and face-rigs are full CBZ.npcs with makeCharacter. The city crowds are shallow: src/city/crowd.js has 360 agents using only 6 InstancedMesh parts (no faces, no inventory, no loot, faceless boxes), and src/city/peds.js has ~90+ NPCs rendered as full rigs but with limited features (no cell-based society sim, no worker, no persistent grudges across demotion). The gap is appearance + data richness: jail agents get skin/hair color, visible valuables, persistent identity + memories + relationships via a Web Worker, while city peds are rendered-only with basic aggression spectrum. The merge target: retire src/city/crowd.js and src/city/peds.js duplication, adopt src/entities/ambientstate.js + src/entities/crowd.js infrastructure (swapping prison zones for city blocks), and optionally attach a lightweight society worker for city NPC autonomy and memory.

keyFiles.role: Jail ambient-tier data model: ~400 typed arrays holding position/velocity/personality/inventory for all agents (TOTAL population). Hidden movement uses route segments; materialize() interpolates position on-demand. Zones [yard0, yard1, south] + 3 personality spectra (nerve, empathy, greed) + role (drifter 0..mediator 5) + faction (+1 gang, 0 neutral, -1 enemy). Skin/hair procedurally chosen. Inventory: cigs + 1 visible valuable (chain=gold, watch=silver, etc). Core: promote(id, now) → moves to instanced-mesh slots; demote(id, now) → back to math. CBZ.ambient = S exported.

Jail render + close-range interaction layer. THREE.InstancedMesh (RIG_CAP≤1600 slots) with 11 meshes per agent (torso orange, head+hair tinted, 4 limbs, eyes/mouth dark, valuable). Selects nearest ~RIG_CAP agents from CBZ.ambient.live via heap-sort distance. Face-rig pool (CROWD_FACE_RIGS≈40) promotes nearest agents into full CBZ.makeCharacter rigs. syncFaceRigs() drives animations + KO/death persistence. chooseNearby() performs LOD selection. Instanced meshes auto-hide when face-rig active (rigOf[id]≥0). Also renders Points cloud (overview mode) + density cells (altitude view). Worker driven from here (society consequences).

Background (hidden) population: off-screen agents in cells, memories (3 slots per agent: GOSSIP/DEAL/THREAT/CALM/PLAYER_SEEN), relationships (3 slots per agent: score ±100), moods, roles → encounter behaviors. Rumormill: lookouts/runners broadcast facts. Traders build deals. Enforcers create threats. Mediators calm. Patches mood/role/memory-kind back to main thread via postMessage. Position snaps rare (every 1.2–6 sec). Heap event queue for scheduled future interactions.

Procedure avatar rig: makeCharacter(c) returns {group, body, neck, head, parts:{ll, rl, la, ra}, sockets:{leftHand, rightHand}, skinSlots:{torso, collar, legs, shoes, arms, hands, head, hair, stripes, belt, badge, cap}, detail:[faces+hair]} + animChar(ch, speed, dt) for layered sine-wave animation (gait, bob, sway, lean, punch). Per-rig unshared materials for emissive flashing on hit. Used by jail NPCs + crowd face-rigs + city peds.

City ambient crowd: simple InstancedMesh tier only (no ambient data layer, no hidden-movement math). 360 agents walking between sidewalk waypoints, NO FACES, NO PERSONALITY, NO LOOT. Six meshes: torso (tinted shirt), head+arms (tinted skin), legL/legR (dark pants). Pure simulation: walk toward goal, repick on arrival, stride swing animation. No worker, no relationships, no visible inventory. Render-only.

City interactive layer: ~90+ full makeCharacter rigs per spawned cohort (civilians, vendors, gang, cops, security). Each has {aggr ∈ [0,1], wealth, armed, weapon, cash, loot, memories (mem=one-shot), state (walk/fight/flee/etc)} but NO PERSISTENT MEMORY SYSTEM (single reference, not matrix). Brain runs per-frame with LOD (ANIM_D2=58², TAG_D2=26², VIS_D2=150², FAR_D2=110²). Time-sliced ai (stride ∝ distance). Think() recomputes from scratch each tick (no agent memories carried across frames in a worker). Move() handles path-finding to intersections + stores + benches.

findings.title: Jail Crowd: Multi-Tier Rendering Architecture
findings.detail: Three-tier rendering: (1) INSTANCEDMESH (11 meshes × RIG_CAP slots): torso/head/hair + 4 limbs + eyes/mouth + valuable item (orange/tinted). Each agent ≤ POINT_CAP (12000 default) auto-rendered with per-instance setColorAt(). Geometry: unit box with white color attr for tinting. (2) Points cloud (overview: altitude ≥220px height): dense cells (DENSITY_W × DENSITY_H grid, 8-unit cells) colored by faction. (3) Face-rig pool (40 agents): promoted to full makeCharacter rigs (cached inside crowd root), repositioned Y=0 (visible) or Y=-1000 (hidden). When promoted, corresponding instanced slot shrinks to 0.0001 scale (hides behind root dummy). Faces are cached pool, reused: see makeFaceEntry @line 122–142, setRigSkin @144–148, assignRig @149–169.

findings.title: Jail Crowd: Appearance Pipeline (Faces → Valuables)
findings.detail: Face-rig assignment: assignRig(e, id) pulls from CBZ.ambient: S.skin[id] (SKIN palette [0xffe0c4...0x573b26], 11 tones), S.hair[id] (8 colors), S.itemColor(id) (visible valuable color or 0). Face has eyes/brow/mouth (all dark, shared material). Head/hair/hands get skin/hair tones via setRigSkin(rig, skin, hair) → rig.head.material.color.setHex(skin), rig.skinSlots.hair[].material.color.setHex(hair). Valuable (chain/watch/tooth/cash) rendered as a 0.36×0.12×0.07 box at chest position (z+0.225), only if S.itemColor(id)≠0 (i.e., item carried). Non-carriers shrink valuable to 0.0001. Instanced crowd (not promoted) show faces via renderRigs(): put(head, slot, 0, 2.18, 0, 0.54, 0.54, 0.54) tinted at S.skin[id], put(valuable, slot, ...) tinted at S.itemColor(id).

findings.title: Jail Crowd: Data Model — Ambient Typed Arrays
findings.detail: CBZ.ambient = S: { total, rigCapacity, zones[], posX/Z, prevX/Z, velX/Z, heading, speed, brainT, panic, avoidX/Z, goal*, route*, zone, role, faction, nerve, empathy, greed, mood, facts, reactivity, grudge, item, cigs, dead, skin, hair, rng, densityCell, densityPopulation, densityFaction, sharedPositionBuffers, sharedPositionMeta }. All Float32Array except role/faction/nerve/empathy/greed/mood/reactivity/grudge/item/cigs/dead/zone (Uint8Array or Int8Array). No shared buffers by default unless canShare (crossOriginIsolated + SharedArrayBuffer). Initialized: zone (random 0–2), speed (1.25+rnd×1.35), role (rnd-weighted 0–5), faction (rnd ±1 or 0), personality (nerve/empathy/greed 0–100), skin/hair from palettes. rollInventory() → item = visible-rare (1=chain, 2=watch, ..., 0=nothing) + cigs (1–7).

findings.title: Jail Crowd: Hidden Movement Logic (Route Segments)
findings.detail: planRoute(id, now, x, z): picks next zone (11% chance) or new point in same zone. Stores routeX0/Z0 (start), routeX1/Z1 (end), routeNextZone, routeStart/Arrival (times). Travel time = max(0.75, dist / speed). materialize(id, now): if not explicit[id], resolves completed routes (loops up to 3 times for large time-skips), then interpolates current position: if arrival > now, t = (now - routeStart) / (arrival - routeStart), posX[id] = routeX0 + (routeX1 − routeX0) × t. Updates density cell. promote(id, now) calls materialize, sets explicit[id]=1, clears avoidance, picks a goal in zone (for the brain loop). demote(id, now) clears explicit[id], calls planRoute to hide the agent. No per-frame movement cost for hidden agents — only materialization + cell updates on query.

findings.title: Jail Crowd: Face-Rig Promotion/Demotion (LOD)
findings.detail: FACE_RIGS constant (40 default), FACE_DIST2 (30² = 900). syncFaceRigs(alpha, dt): (1) scan activeId list (promoted agents within FACE_DIST2 of player) → collect in faceTmpId/faceTmpD2 heap. (2) release rigs whose agent dropped out of range: freeRig(e) moves rig.position.y to FAR_AWAY (-1000), restores S.posX/Z from rig position (persist movement), clears rig.group.visible, marks rigOf[id]=-1. (3) assign free rigs: assignRig(e, id) pulls S.skin/hair/item/name/loadout/nerve/greed/reactivity/grudge/downT, paints rig, flags synced=false. (4) drive assigned rigs: if a.dead, ragdoll (corpseT-=dt, set visible=false on timeout), else if a.ko, lay down (rotation.z→π/2), else upright + animate. Casting a.chain.visible based on S.itemColor(id). Persist grudge/downT back: S.grudge[id] = max(S.grudge[id], a.playerGrudge×32), S.downT[id] = max(...).

findings.title: Jail Crowd: Appearance in Instanced View (Non-Promoted)
findings.detail: renderRigs(alpha): for each activeCount agent (except those with rigOf[id]≥0, which are hidden), render 11 per-instance matrices (torso at local 0,1.42,0; head at 0,2.18,0 tinted S.skin[id]; hair at 0,2.50,0 tinted S.hair[id]; legs+arms with swing; eyes/mouth at face positions; valuable at chest). Heads/hair use tintUnit (BoxGeometry + white color attribute) so setColorAt() multiplies properly. Valuables only rendered if vcol=S.itemColor(id)≠0, else scale to 0.0001. Eyes/mouth always dark (no per-instance tint). This gives every instanced agent the appearance of a small colored head + body, not faceless boxes.

findings.title: City Crowd: Appearance Pipeline (Faceless Boxes)
findings.detail: City crowd: 360 agents, 6 InstancedMesh parts (torso, head, armL, armR, legL, legR), NO FACES (no eyes, no mouth), NO VISIBLE VALUABLES, NO INVENTORY. Geometries: tintBox() = BoxGeometry + white color attribute for tinting. torso = 0.6×0.8×0.34 tinted shirt color (SHIRTS[shirt[i]]). head = 0.36×0.36×0.36 tinted skin color. arms 0.15×0.62×0.15 tinted skin. legs 0.2×0.78×0.22 dark pants (no tint, single global color 0x2c3038). render(): for i in 0..count, setPart() constructs per-agent matrix (position px[i]/pz[i], scale 1), stride swing = sin(phase[i])×0.5 applied to arms/legs. No bob-animation for head (only torso+bob for simple rotation). Result: boxes with a shirt, a skin-tone head, skin arms, dark legs. No person-ness (no face, no inventory, no identity).

findings.title: City Peds: Character Appearance (Full Rigs, Faceless Inventory)
findings.detail: makePed(x, z, r, opts) calls makeCharacter({legs, torso, collar, arms, skin, hair, shoes}) → full rig with animations. Each ped has {outfit (torso color), skin, hair (8 colors), aggr, wealth, armed, weapon (Pistol/Carbine), cash, loot (single item, not array)}. No visible jewelry/chain on ped (unlike jail), so loot is invisible (brain-only, not rendered). move() animates at near distance (d2 < ANIM_D2=58²), LOD freezes animation beyond. body.hit() for knockdown physics. Death ragdolls via physics (owner: ped, not crowd system). Individual NPCs but no persistent social graph; mem is a single reference to one offender, refreshed each cycle.

findings.title: Jail Society Worker: Memory + Relationship Persistence
findings.detail: Off-screen population: crowd-worker.js maintains per-agent: memories (3 slots × total: memoryKind, memoryStrength 0–100, memoryTTL in sec, memorySource id, memoryX/Z quantized), relationships (3 slots × total: relationId, relationScore ±100), mood 0–100, facts (witness/dealer/injured/faction-action bitmask). Encounter(a, b, weight): if trader-role, forge deals; if enforcer, create threats; if mediator, calm; else gossip rumor. Rumormill spreads strongest memory via lookouts/runners. playerWitness(id): if player armed/hot within 13 units, agent records PLAYER_SEEN memory (mind you, even hidden agents record this when pos snapped to worker). decay(id): mood drifts toward 50; memories expire (memoryTTL-=drift). Grid-cell spatial index (9×9 units) for fast encounter sampling. Event heap for scheduled future interactions. Main thread snaps positions every 1.2–6 sec (sync via sharedPositionBuffers if available). Worker patches back mood/role/memory-kind every patch.

findings.title: Instancing Approach: Jail vs City
findings.detail: Jail: 11 InstancedMesh meshes per body (discrete parts: torso, head, hair, 4 limbs, eyes, mouth, valuable). Each mesh has its own instanceMatrix + (if tinted) instanceColor buffer. Per-instance tint via setColorAt(mesh, slot, color) for skin/hair/valuable. Eyes/mouth shared dark material (no per-instance color). renderRigs() builds compound per-agent transform (bob, swing, bob-pitch) applied to rootDummy, then multiplies per-part local matrices to get world position per part per slot. Expensive: 11 matrix uploads per agent per frame, but enables rich visual detail + inventory display. City: 6 InstancedMesh (torso, head, armL, armR, legL, legR). Single global material per part (shirt for torso, skin for head/arms, dark for legs). Per-instance tint only on torso/head/arms (pantsMat has no instanceColor). Simpler: 6 matrices per agent, but no face detail, no item visibility. Jail meshes scale to 0.0001 when promoted to face-rigs; city has no such hierarchy.

findings.title: LOD (Levels of Detail) Strategy
findings.detail: Jail: (1) hidden agents (explicit=0): no render, no update. (2) active agents (slotOf[id]≥0, promoted): InstancedMesh render + per-frame physics/animation. (3) face agents (rigOf[id]≥0, within FACE_DIST2): full rig visible, synced every frame. Visibility: meshes.visible = !overview (overview mode hides instanced rigs, shows points/density instead). Nearby selection: chooseNearby() max-heaps DESIRED_RIGS agents by distance; demotes far ones, promotes near ones. City: (1) tag visible iff d2 < TAG_D2=26². (2) animation iff d2 < ANIM_D2=58² OR important (ped.rage || ped.guard || controlled || npcWanted || armed). (3) group visible iff d2 < VIS_D2=150² OR important. (4) brain time-sliced: stride=(frame+ped.slice)%stride, stride=active?4:(far?20:10). No separate face-rig tier; all rigs are interactable but unshared. No hidden-math tier; all AI reruns per-frame.

findings.title: Inventory Visibility
findings.detail: Jail: visible valuables rendered as a box (0.34×0.12×0.07) at the chest (y=1.60, z=0.225) IF S.itemColor(id)≠0. Color-coded: 0xffd451=gold chain, 0xd6dde8=silver watch, 0xffe27a=gold tooth, 0x57c264=green cash. Instanced crowd shows this directly (put(valuable, slot, ...)). Face-rig wears a _chain mesh (similar box) at rig.body position (1.46, 0.26) colored per item. Easy loot hook: see a guy with a gold chain, steal from him. City: NO VISIBLE INVENTORY. Ped.loot/armed/weapon/cash all brain-only. Interaction menu reveals them (talk/rob via CBZ.cityRobPed which reads ped.cash, ped.loot). Appearance motivation is purely cosmetic (outfit color), not economic signal.

findings.title: Performance: Ambient Math Cost vs Render Cost
findings.detail: Jail: (1) Main thread: fixedStep(FIXED=0.05) physics for active agents only (~100–400 agents), no per-frame rethink. Worker runs off-thread (hidden agents). Overhead ∝ activeCount, not TOTAL. (2) Worker: event-driven encounters (contacts scheduled by grid scan, not all-pairs). Memory slots 3×TOTAL but sparse updates (only changed agents patch back). (3) Render: 11 InstancedMesh uploads if overview, else just matrix updates (fast). City: (1) Main thread: move(ped, dt, animate) + think(ped, dt, active) for EVERY ped every frame, sliced stride=(frame+slice)%(active?4:far?20:10). Overhead ∝ CBZ.cityPeds.length. (2) No worker. (3) Render: every rig visible updates per frame (no hidden math). Cost: brain reruns ≈ ~300 peds × (1/4 to 1/20) ≈ 75–300 think() calls/frame. For >500 peds, city grid becomes expensive. Jail scales to 6000+ with hidden math free.

mergePlan.step: 1. Duplicate src/entities/ambientstate.js into src/city/ambientstate.js, adapt zones to city blocks (8×8 blocks covering city arena), replace ZONES array with city block bounds, set MASS_CROWD to city population (e.g., 6000), initialize per-block densities instead of yard 3-zone.
mergePlan.risk: Arena bounds must be known at module load time (or lazy-init ambientstate after buildCity()). SKIN/HAIR/FIRST/LAST palettes can stay (reused from jail). RNG must seed per mode to avoid dups between escape/city runs. Initial spawn logic: instead of randomPoint(id, zoneId), call arena.randomSidewalkPoint().

mergePlan.step: 2. Create src/city/crowd.js (new, replacing old), adapt entities/crowd.js logic: read ambientstate from city namespace (CBZ.city.ambient instead of CBZ.ambient), use same InstancedMesh 11-part pipeline, but remove worker integration (city has no background simulation layer yet; can add later). Keep chooseNearby(), syncFaceRigs(), renderRigs() identical. Face-rig pool reuses makeCharacter. Change isEscape = CBZ.game.mode==='escape' to isCity = CBZ.game.mode==='city'.
mergePlan.risk: Face-rig promotion logic must map city ambient agents to city peds (CBZ.cityPeds). Currently CBZ.npcs (escape mode). Create CBZ.city.peds = [] or rename CBZ.cityPeds to be the face-rig pool. Interaction systems already expect CBZ.city.peds or cityPeds; no change needed if we use the same property.

mergePlan.step: 3. Retire old src/city/peds.js ambient-only crowd spawner (CBZ.spawnCityCrowd, the 360 faceless boxes), fold its role into new crowd.js: CBZ.spawnCityCrowd() now calls CBZ.city.ambient.init() and builds the instanced-mesh layer. Old CBZ.cityMakePed() (the full-rig spawner) stays but receives assignments from syncFaceRigs() instead of standalone spawn loops.
mergePlan.risk: CBZ.spawnCityPeds() spawns ~80 peds + vendors + gangs + cops. These are NOT the ambient crowd; they're named/special roles. Keep them. Only retire the 360-agent ambient spawn that city/crowd.js currently owns.

mergePlan.step: 4. Adapt face-rig assignment to city context: in new crowd.js syncFaceRigs(), instead of promoting to CBZ.npcs, promote to CBZ.cityPeds (the named peds + ambient promotions). assignRig(e, id) should: fetch ambient data (name via S.nameOf(id), skin/hair/item/personality via S array), check if CBZ.cityPeds already has an entry for this id (marked _crowd=true), else create a stub ped entry mimicking CBZ.cityMakePed() output but without the full init.
mergePlan.risk: City peds have {aggr, wealth, armed, weapon, gang, guard} that ambient agents don't have. Decide: (a) derive aggr from ambient personality (nerve/empathy/greed combo → aggression score), or (b) roll random aggr on promote. Option (a) is richer: high greed + low empathy → high aggr (crook/violent). Ambient data must expand or crowd.js must compute.

mergePlan.step: 5. Swap rendering: new crowd.js renders ambient instanced-mesh tier (torso+head+hair+limbs+eyes+mouth+valuable), and face-rigs render via their full makeCharacter rigs. Turn OFF old city/crowd.js ambient 360-agent rendering (6-part faceless boxes). Verify InstancedMesh visibility gating (overview mode, render distance, promoted→hide instanced slot).
mergePlan.risk: Overview/density mode may not exist in city (city has no simView). Simplify: overview modes off, always render instanced+face-rigs. Or adapt simView for city if desired (altitude overview of blocks).

mergePlan.step: 6. Adapt interaction flow: face-rig peds now carry ambient identity (S.nameOf(id), S.skin[id], S.item[id]). When player interacts (city/interact.js), query the ped._id to fetch ambient inventory (S.cigs[ped._id], S.item[ped._id]). Loot/rob now reads ambient arrays. Murder sets S.dead[id]=1, preventing re-promotion. Grudges persist via S.grudge[id].
mergePlan.risk: Old city logic reads ped.cash, ped.loot, ped.name directly. New logic must dual-source: if ped._crowd, read ambient S[]; else read ped.* (for named vendors/gangs). Transition: mark all promoted peds _crowd=true, all hand-spawned peds _crowd=false.

mergePlan.step: 7. Adapt brain/movement: old city/peds.js think() + move() runs full brain each frame for all CBZ.cityPeds. With new crowd, keep hand-spawned peds (vendors, cops, named gang members) running full brain, but DEMOTE non-essential peds if far from player (fixedStep lod, like jail). Or: run movement in main ambient arrays (posX/Z/velX/Z updated every frame via simple physics), sync movement back from arrays to ped.group.position on render.
mergePlan.risk: Ambient array physics (velocity integration) must use city arena colliders (not jail obstacles). Arena.js must export collider list. Current peds.js does not integrate physics (only sets pos via path-following); adopting ambient physics is a behavior change (inertia, collide() calls).

mergePlan.step: 8. (Optional) Add lightweight city society worker: fork workers/crowd-worker.js into src/city/crowd-worker.js, strip out jail role-specific behaviors (trader/enforcer/mediator), keep basic memory + relationship matrix + grid scan for encounters. City focus: aggr-spectrum reactions (meek talks, bold stands ground, crook mobs, violent fights), gang turf conflicts, witness reporting.
mergePlan.risk: City NPC counts (500+) will fill worker memory fast. Cap hidden agents or purge old memories aggressively. Worker overhead ∝ visible peds (grid scan), not total, so scales reasonably. Opt-in via CBZ.CITY.crowdWorker flag.

mergePlan.step: 9. Update index.html boot order: move new src/city/ambientstate.js load to BEFORE src/city/mode.js (so CBZ.city.ambient exists). Move new src/city/crowd.js AFTER src/city/peds.js (crowd depends on makeCharacter + scene). Retire old city/crowd.js script tag.
mergePlan.risk: Boot order is critical. Test in headless + browser to catch undefined references.

mergePlan.step: 10. Testing + perf validation: (a) Spawn city with N=1000 agents, verify frame rate (target 60 fps with ~100 face-rigs visible). (b) Interact with a ped, verify inventory reads ambient S.cigs/S.item. (c) Loot a ped, verify S.dead[id]=1 persists. (d) Switch modes escape↔city, verify ambient stays/resets. (e) Visual regression: city peds should now have visible faces (eyes+brow+mouth) + skin-tone heads + visible valuables. (f) Perf: ambient physics loop (fixedStep) << instanced render (11 matrices per agent); measure CBZ.crowdPerformance.simTimeMs vs renderTimeMs.
mergePlan.risk: Face appearance in city may surprise (peds look different). Item visibility (valuables on chest) changes robbery tactics (see the rich marks). City brain time-slicing (stride) is new, may cause lag spikes if poorly tuned.
